text.title: Structured Logging and Alert Rules
text.summary: Structured JSON logging with request_id, Prometheus alert rule patterns, and burn-rate alerting guidance.

rule.statement: Every log line must carry a request_id field derived from the middleware, bound at the start of the request and propagated through all nested calls.
rule.rationale: Without a request_id correlator, tracing a single failing request across dozens of log lines is guesswork. It is the minimum for diagnosing incidents.

rule.statement: Use structured logging (structlog, python-json-logger) emitting JSON. Never use string formatting to build log messages that mix context and message.
rule.rationale: JSON logs are machine-parseable by Loki, Datadog, and CloudWatch. String-formatted logs require fragile regex parsing.

rule.statement: Use burn-rate alerts (multi-window, Google SRE workbook) instead of static threshold alerts. Define burn-rate relative to your SLO error budget.
rule.rationale: Static threshold alerts fire too late (slow burn) or too often (noisy with short spikes). Burn-rate alerts detect fast burns immediately and slow burns within the SLO window.

example.code:
groups: - name: api_alerts rules: - alert: HighErrorRate expr: rate(http_requests_total{status=~"5.."}[5m]) / rate(http_requests_total[5m]) > 0.01 for: 5m labels: severity: critical annotations: summary: "High error rate detected (>1%)"
- alert: SlowResponses expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(http_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) > 1 for: 10m labels: severity: warning annotations: summary: "P95 latency above 1s for 10 minutes"

antipattern.description: Adding request_id or user_id as a Prometheus label.
antipattern.reason: Cardinality explosion: each unique ID is a new time series; Prometheus OOMs within hours on moderate traffic.

antipattern.description: Double-logging: both middleware and handler log the same request.
antipattern.reason: Inflates log volume 2x; costs and noise increase with no diagnostic benefit.

text.reference: Prometheus alert rules for error rate and latency SLOs
